verdict.answer_first: A states the action immediately: “Look at the actual SQL the endpoint runs”; B opens with a debatable frame: “2M rows is small.”
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one path: “Find the slow query first” then “Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE”; B surveys more layers before landing on the same step.
verdict.fabrication: B invents unsupported anchors: “single-digit milliseconds,” “30-second check,” and “80% of the time”; A has softer but still broad claims like “almost always.”
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives a clean order and concrete tells: slow query logging/APM, `EXPLAIN ANALYZE`, `Seq Scan`, indexes, N+1, OFFSET.
